what_it_does: Records a boundary rule for the dragnet-development channel: Obsidian is limited to presentation and intake/proposals, while the fabric owns identity, edges, resolution, and the minting of canonical records.
observations: Obsidian is allowed to render and to propose edits or annotations
Only cc_board and resolve_address may mint a canonical record or edge
The vault is downstream of the board on reads and upstream-only-as-proposal on writes
Reading a file move, rename, or wikilink back as canonical is treated as a boundary violation
The item is filed as a block with no thread in channel dragnet-development
The title presents the rule as a one-line version of a larger boundary discussion
standouts: The crisp one-line boundary: Obsidian renders and proposes; the fabric owns identity, edges, resolution, and truth
The explicit anti-pattern that Obsidian must not become master through read-back of file moves, renames, or wikilinks
concerns: cc_board and resolve_address are named but not defined in this file
The phrase minting of truth is metaphorical and lacks operational detail here
No mechanism is shown for how proposals are accepted, rejected, or transformed into canonical records
notes: A concise policy item that draws the line between Obsidian as a presentation/proposal layer and the fabric as the canonical minting authority.
questions: What is the exact proposal validation and acceptance workflow?
How are conflicts between Obsidian proposals and existing canonical records resolved?
What does minting of truth mean in concrete system terms?
Are there bootstrap or offline-edit exceptions to the upstream-only-as-proposal rule?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to state where the boundary sits in one line, and the file does that while adding concrete elaboration and an anti-pattern, so purpose and actual align.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between user-facing tools and canonical backend
Authority and ownership of canonical state
Downstream read path versus upstream proposal write path
Guardrails against tool capture of canonical truth
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent Obsidian from silently becoming the source of truth by making the boundary memorable and listing the specific operations that must remain fabric-owned.
novelty: The one-line version framing plus a short list of concrete boundary violations turns an architectural principle into an easily referenced policy snippet.
contribution: A reusable, compact boundary statement that a fused system can keep as a policy reference for dividing presentation/proposal responsibilities from canonical minting responsibilities.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item defining the system boundary so that Obsidian handles presentation and proposals while cc_board and resolve_address alone mint canonical records and edges.
intention_for_embedding: To establish and communicate the canonical-authority boundary between Obsidian and the fabric in a single memorable formulation.
